title: Fideo Seco Dry Mexican Noodles

seo.title: Fideo Seco Recipe: Authentic Dry Mexican Noodles
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make the best Fideo Seco recipe at home! This authentic dry Mexican noodle dish is easy, flavorful, and perfect for any meal. Get the step-by-step guide now!
seo.h1: Authentic Fideo Seco (Dry Mexican Noodles) Recipe
h2Headings: How to Make Fideo Seco: Step-by-Step Instructions
Tips for the Best Dry Mexican Noodles
seo.imageAlt: A vibrant bowl of Fideo Seco, dry Mexican noodles, garnished with cilantro and queso fresco, ready to serve.
seo.imageCaption: Our delicious Fideo Seco recipe brings the authentic flavors of Mexico right to your kitchen. Perfect as a side or a light main!

description: Fideo Seco is a cherished Mexican dish featuring toasted fideo noodles simmered in a rich, flavorful tomato sauce until dry and perfectly tender. This comforting 'dry soup' is a staple in many Mexican homes, often enjoyed as a side or a light main course. Its simple yet profound flavors make it a beloved recipe for all ages.

instructions.title: Prepare the Tomato Sauce
instructions.text: In a blender, combine the halved Roma tomatoes, roughly chopped white onion, and peeled garlic cloves with 1/2 cup of the chicken broth. Blend until completely smooth. Set aside. This blend creates the flavorful base for your Fideo Seco.

instructions.title: Toast the Fideo Noodles
instructions.text: Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the fideo noodles and cook, stirring constantly, until they turn a deep golden brown. This step is crucial for developing the nutty flavor and preventing the noodles from becoming mushy.

instructions.title: Add the Tomato Sauce
instructions.text: Carefully pour the blended tomato sauce into the skillet with the toasted fideo noodles. Be cautious as the sauce may splatter. Stir well to coat all the noodles and bring the mixture to a simmer. Cook for about 2-3 minutes, allowing the sauce to thicken slightly.

instructions.title: Simmer with Broth
instructions.text: Pour in the remaining 2 1/2 cups of chicken broth. Add salt and black pepper to taste. Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover the skillet, and let it simmer. The noodles will absorb the liquid and cook through.

instructions.title: Finish Cooking and Rest
instructions.text: Continue to simmer, stirring occasionally, until most of the liquid has been absorbed by the noodles and they are tender, approximately 10-12 minutes. Remove the skillet from the heat and let it rest, covered, for 5 minutes. This allows the flavors to meld and the noodles to finish cooking perfectly.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Fluff the Fideo Seco with a fork. Serve hot, garnished generously with crumbled queso fresco, fresh chopped cilantro, and a drizzle of crema Mexicana or sour cream. It makes a wonderful side dish or a light main course.

tips: Always toast your fideo noodles to a deep golden brown. This step prevents them from getting mushy and gives the dish its signature nutty flavor.
For an extra kick, add a small piece of chipotle pepper in adobo sauce to your tomato blend. Adjust the amount to your preferred spice level.
Fideo Seco is best served immediately, but leftovers can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. Reheat gently on the stovetop with a splash of broth if needed.
article: Fideo Seco Dry Mexican Noodles is a beloved Mexican Pasta and Noodles dish enjoyed across Mexican. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
